content.topic_intro: File I/O means reading data from files and writing data into files.
content.why_learn_this: Without file handling, programs cannot save user data, settings, reports, logs, or application information permanently.

explanations.text: File I/O helps programs read and write files.
Saving a game, storing notes, or downloading reports all use file handling.
Files help programs remember information even after the app closes.
File I/O allows Java applications to interact with external storage systems.
File handling enables persistent data management through structured input and output streams connected to the file system.

creating_file.title: Creating a File
creating_file.real_life: Apps create settings and save files.

writing_file.title: Writing Into File

reading_file.title: Reading File Data
reading_file.real_life: Reading saved notes and user data.

append_data.title: Appending Data
append_data.explanation: true allows adding data without deleting old content.

exception_handling.title: Why try-catch is Important
exception_handling.explanation: File operations may fail if file does not exist or access is denied.
exception_handling.real_life: Apps should not crash if file errors happen.

mini_project.title: Simple Notes App
mini_project.task: Create a program that saves notes into a file and reads saved notes back to the user.

revision_summary: Files help programs store permanent data.
